Survey: Elephant in the Room: Nice White Ladies Encore and More (Part 1): Elephant in the Room: Nice White Ladies Encore and More (Part 1)
Answer: During the session several people dropped off and there were multiple and repeated comments about the reason behind it.  While it may have been true that people were uncomfortable and unable to process the content, it wasn't appropriate to engage in shaming.  That isn't productive for the folks new to the journey of engaging in their own contribution to racism and may have an opposite impact.  The speakers could have chosen to address it in a more trauma informed way that called out that disengaging is a coping mechanism but the seeds are sown.  How we who choose to stay engaged treat those in the beginning of the journey MATTERS.  Grace is important.  I wasn't able to join on day 2 due to a scheduling conflict and one of my colleagues hinted that "they will think you are scared".  That really isn't how we want to do this work, is it?  And no, I wasn't scared, and wanted to join, I simply couldn't. Maybe that is why people left Part 1? Never underestimate where the seeds may have landed.

It is important to empower parents to know/see they can make a change- it is ultimately there choice by WE (CW) are doing are very best to ensure they can make changes needed to be safe and sober.
